metadata.title: Smart Environments Exam Quiz Data
metadata.study_mode: short active recall, formulas, cloze, multiple choice

formula_cards.explanation: Sampling period is the time between samples; it is the reciprocal of sampling frequency.
To reconstruct a band-limited signal, sample at least twice the maximum frequency W.

formula_cards.back: W is the highest frequency component in the signal.
formula_cards.explanation: If the fastest component is 5 kHz, then W = 5 kHz and minimum f_s = 10 kHz.

formula_cards.back: X(f) = ∫ x(t)e^{-j2πft} dt over all time
formula_cards.explanation: Represents a signal as a weighted sum of complex sinusoids.

formula_cards.front: What is j in the Fourier transform?
formula_cards.back: j = sqrt(-1), the imaginary unit used in engineering.
formula_cards.explanation: Engineers use j instead of i because i is often used for current.

formula_cards.front: sinc definition used in the notes
formula_cards.explanation: Because this convention lacks π inside sinc, the reconstruction argument should include π.
Continuous signal reconstructed as a sum of shifted sinc interpolation functions.

formula_cards.front: Number of quantization levels from B bits
formula_cards.explanation: B bits can represent 2^B amplitude levels.

formula_cards.front: Quantization step size for range [-A, A]
formula_cards.explanation: Δ is the spacing between neighboring quantization levels.
With rounding to nearest level, the maximum absolute error is half a quantization step.
For uniform quantization error, distortion is proportional to the square of the step size.
More bits B reduce distortion exponentially.
If desired distortion D decreases, required bits B increase.
The degree of a node is the sum of its adjacency weights.
The graph Laplacian describes graph connectivity.
When A acts on eigenvector v, it only scales it by eigenvalue λ.
Projects a graph signal onto the Laplacian eigenvector basis.
Reconstructs the graph signal from graph-frequency coefficients.
A 3D point projects to a 2D image; increasing depth Z makes the projection smaller.
Divide by 2 because the light travels to the object and back.
Convolution computes weighted sums of neighboring signal values.

formula_cards.back: x*h in time/space corresponds to X(f)H(f) in frequency.
formula_cards.explanation: Filtering by convolution becomes multiplication in the frequency domain.
Higher spreading factor means longer symbol time and lower bitrate, but better sensitivity/range.

concept_cards.back: A physical environment enriched with sensors, actuators, computation, and communication so it can monitor conditions, process data, decide, and act.

concept_cards.front: Smart flow: put the chain in order.
concept_cards.back: Sensors → data/database → information processing → decision algorithms → services/actuators → physical environment.
Small autonomous networked devices that interact with the physical world, humans, or other devices.
A network of physical objects embedded with sensors, electronics, software, and connectivity, enabling them to collect/exchange data and receive/react to commands.
Energy constraints, security, privacy, performance/reliability, interoperability, scalability.

concept_cards.front: What does 'Anything + Communications = Smart Anything' mean?
concept_cards.back: Adding sensing/communication/processing to ordinary objects turns them into smart applications such as smart homes, smart cars, or smart healthcare.

concept_cards.front: Data path elements in smart environments.
concept_cards.back: Data acquisition, local data processing, data communication, data stream, data storage/cloud, data analytics.
Sensor design/embedding, density, accuracy, tunability, energy efficiency, cost, privacy, environmental concerns.
Balance local vs remote processing and computation vs communication; local processing can reduce data transmission but consumes processing energy.
Reliability, latency, throughput, security, energy efficiency, mobility, architecture, range/coverage, bandwidth.

concept_cards.front: What is context/location awareness useful for?
concept_cards.back: It allows devices to infer user activity/location and adapt information content and distribution.
Non-3GPP: LoRa, Sigfox, Weightless, others. 3GPP: LTE-M, EC-GSM, NB-IoT, 5G.
Adaptive traffic lights, multiservice public lighting, smart bins, people-flow sensing, pollution monitoring, building security, structural health monitoring, energy/resource optimization.
Structural health of roads/bridges/tunnels, traffic flow, parking availability, environmental conditions, intelligent parking, EV recharging.

concept_cards.front: LoRaWAN smart city use cases in the slides.
concept_cards.back: Smart metering, smart bins, LoRaWAN signposts, water metering, parking management, smart buildings, edible oil recycling, port logistics.

concept_cards.front: Parking monitoring: what data is collected?
concept_cards.back: Lot occupancy status, time spent on stalls, occupancy times by type and time slot, individual or area-level statistics.
Sensors measure container fill levels; digitized data helps route trucks efficiently and supports circular-economy collection.
Replaces manual reading, reduces water waste, helps utilities/cities manage consumption, and can provide hourly insights.
A wireless local area network that uses radio waves as its carrier; usually the last link to users is wireless while backbone is wired.
Wireless hosts, base stations/access points, wireless links, and network infrastructure.

concept_cards.front: Infrastructure mode vs ad hoc mode.
concept_cards.back: Infrastructure: base station connects mobiles to wired network. Ad hoc: no base station; nodes organize and route among themselves.
Single-hop vs multi-hop, and infrastructure vs no infrastructure.

concept_cards.front: Why are wireless links harder than wired links?
concept_cards.back: Signal attenuation/path loss, interference from other sources, and multipath propagation.
Higher SNR makes it easier to extract the signal and usually decreases BER.

concept_cards.front: Given a fixed SNR, how should the physical layer be chosen?
concept_cards.back: Choose the modulation/rate that meets the BER requirement while giving the highest throughput.

concept_cards.front: High frequency wireless: benefit and drawback.
concept_cards.back: Higher frequency can support higher data transfer, but obstacles/path loss become more problematic.
A and C cannot hear each other but both can interfere at B, so they are unaware of a collision at B.

concept_cards.front: Physical layer vs data link layer.
concept_cards.back: Physical layer transmits raw bits. Data link layer manages medium access/collision avoidance such as CSMA/CA.
An organization that creates standards for protocols such as Wi-Fi/802.11.
Most WLAN products operate in unlicensed bands; 2.4 GHz is common, and 5 GHz is used by 802.11a.

concept_cards.front: Frequency hopping vs direct sequence spread spectrum.
concept_cards.back: FH hops carrier frequency according to a known pattern; DS keeps a fixed carrier band and spreads data over a wider frequency range.
Uses OFDM, operates around 5 GHz, up to 54 Mbps, less interference but shorter range and poorer compatibility with 802.11b.
802.11b: 2.4 GHz, DSSS, up to 11 Mbps, longer range. 802.11a: 5 GHz, OFDM, up to 54 Mbps, shorter range.
High-speed extension to 802.11b: up to 54 Mbps, 2.4 GHz, backward compatible.
Authentication, confidentiality, and integrity, but WEP is vulnerable and uses weak/static keys.
A planning activity to define coverage areas, data rates, AP placement, signal strength, SNR, and RF interference.

concept_cards.front: Why are communications needed in IoT?
concept_cards.back: To exchange information between sensors, communicate with a remote sink/cloud, and enable remote control.

concept_cards.front: Classical short-range IoT technologies: pros and cons.
concept_cards.back: Pros: low cost, low power, unlicensed. Cons: limited distance and need for local coordinator/hub/AP.

concept_cards.front: Cellular WAN IoT: pros and cons.
concept_cards.back: Pros: long range. Cons: higher device cost, complex protocols, licensed spectrum and operation cost.
Low-Power Wide Area Network: combines low energy/low cost with long-range coverage for low-rate IoT traffic.
Long battery life, massive number of devices, extended coverage, low device cost, low deployment cost.
End devices communicate to LPWAN gateways; gateways forward over backhaul to network/application servers and cloud/customer IT.
LoRaWAN, Sigfox, Ingenu RPMA, Weightless/other, and cellular IoT such as LTE-M, NB-IoT, EC-GSM.
LoRa is the physical modulation/link layer; LoRaWAN is the network protocol/system architecture built on top of LoRa.
LoRaWAN is LPWAN; LoRa uses Chirp Spread Spectrum, typical 125 kHz bandwidth, high sensitivity, long range, good indoor penetration.
A frequency-modulated pulse whose frequency changes monotonically during its duration: up-chirp rises, down-chirp falls.
Information is encoded by shifting chirps in time/frequency slots; the number of possible shifts depends on the spreading factor.

concept_cards.front: What does increasing spreading factor do?
concept_cards.back: Increases sensitivity and range/penetration but decreases bitrate and increases time-on-air/energy/collision risk.
SF7: higher bitrate, lower sensitivity, for close/good signal. SF12: lower bitrate, higher sensitivity, for far/deep-indoor/poor signal.
LoRaWAN behavior is close to pure ALOHA; channel capacity usage is limited and collisions can drop many packets under load.
Device listens only after its own uplink transmission; most energy-efficient; suitable for battery devices with no latency constraint.
Device opens scheduled receive windows synchronized by beacons; optimized energy with bounded latency.
Device is almost always listening except during transmissions; lowest latency but high power use, suitable for grid-powered devices.
ABP: fixed addresses/keys, simple but vulnerable after reset. OTAA: join procedure with new session keys, supports roaming.
First LPWAN; ultra-narrowband, proprietary, low throughput (~100 bps), low power, long range, 140 messages/day/device, subscription/cloud model.

concept_cards.front: Main difference: LoRaWAN vs Sigfox gateways.
concept_cards.back: With LoRaWAN you can deploy your own gateways; Sigfox is a proprietary operator/subscription network.
Uplink payload is about 12 bytes; downlink payload is about 8 bytes; each uplink message is transmitted 3 times.
LTE-M/eMTC, NB-IoT, EC-GSM-IoT.
Evolution of LTE optimized for IoT: low power, easy deployment, interoperable with LTE, up to about 1 Mbps, uses legacy cellular infrastructure.
A 3GPP cellular IoT technology optimized for low-end IoT with narrow bandwidth, low power, and licensed-spectrum QoS.
GSM/EDGE enhancements for IoT, leveraging existing GSM coverage, with low data rates and extended coverage.
Vehicular Ad Hoc Network: vehicles and roadside units communicate, often without central access points, for safety and traffic applications.
Improve safety, reduce congestion/fuel waste, support traffic efficiency, and add high-tech vehicle services.
Single-hop IVC is for short-range applications; multihop IVC supports longer-range communication but is more complex.
DSRC uses the 5.9 GHz band, approximately 5.850–5.925 GHz.
Vehicles have high but predictable mobility, dynamic topology, no severe power constraint, and accurate localization via GPS/sensors.
Buildings and obstacles, interference, high speed, changing topology, city/highway differences, and 2D road topology.
Attackers can be insider/outsider, malicious/rational, active/passive, local/extended.
Bogus traffic information, intelligent collisions, identity/speed/position cheating, jamming, tunnel, tracking.
Based on IEEE 802.11a ideas, uses OFDM and supports BPSK, QPSK, 16-QAM, 64-QAM with coding rates.
CSMA/CD detects collisions after they occur; CSMA/CA tries to avoid collisions before transmission, used in wireless.

concept_cards.front: Why is CSMA/CD unsuitable for wireless?
concept_cards.back: Wireless nodes cannot reliably detect collisions while transmitting, so collision avoidance is preferred.
Two nodes cannot hear each other but both can interfere at a receiver, causing collision.
A node unnecessarily refrains from transmitting because it hears a nearby transmission, even though its transmission would not interfere with the intended receiver.
Request-to-Send/Clear-to-Send handshaking helps reserve the medium and reduce hidden-terminal collisions.
Distributed Coordination Function: CSMA/CA-based MAC with contention/backoff and optional RTS/CTS.
High density needs longer backoff to avoid collisions; low density with too long backoff wastes time.
Broadcast spreads traffic warnings such as detour routes, accident alerts, construction warnings, and safety messages.
Contention and collisions caused by excessive/redundant rebroadcasting in a broadcasting network.
Probability-based, location-based, neighbor-based, cluster-based.
Selects forwarding nodes based on how much additional area they cover, possibly using position prediction.
Nodes are grouped into clusters; elected cluster-heads forward packets.
To transmit data from nodes to server, server to nodes, and between nodes; simple IoT data should not need heavy file/email protocols.
MQTT, CoAP, HTTP, XMPP, AMQP.
A well-understood application protocol for client-server data transfer; traverses firewalls/NAT easily because clients initiate connections.
GET read page, HEAD read header, POST append, PUT store, DELETE remove, TRACE echo, CONNECT proxy, OPTIONS query options.
A lightweight client-server publish/subscribe messaging transport protocol, designed for high volumes of data in low-bandwidth networks.
Clients publish messages to topics; the broker forwards messages to clients subscribed to those topics.
Connection, authentication, communication, termination.
At most once: PUBLISH. At least once: PUBLISH/PUBACK. Exactly once: PUBLISH/PUBREC and PUBREL/PUBCOMP.
Scalability with tree/topic structure, interoperability because payloads are binary and app-specific, security requiring authentication and TLS.
Application-layer protocol providing bidirectional full-duplex communication over a single TCP connection.
Bidirectional, full-duplex, single TCP connection, efficient implementation.
XML describes and focuses on what data is; HTML displays and focuses on how data looks.
Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol: XML-based protocol for near-real-time exchange of structured extensible data.
Decentralized client-server architecture: clients do not talk directly, there is no central authoritative server, anyone can run a server.
Originally XMPP on TCP; now many messages over HTTP; WebSockets are an alternative.
On-demand compute services over the Internet, including IaaS, PaaS, SaaS, and Security as a Service.
Public cloud and private cloud; public cloud may suffer from noisy-neighbor effects when services share hardware.
Sensors collecting data, CPU/MCU processing locally, gateway facilitating communication, remote server receiving commands/data and providing analytics/visualization.
Computer security matters because attacks may be physical, financial, or reputational; use encryption and authentication between client/server.
Process of cleansing, transforming, and modeling data to discover useful information, suggest conclusions, and support decision-making.
Collection, ingestion, discovery & cleansing, integration, analysis, delivery.
Bad/multitude sensor data, huge volume requiring decentralized/parallel processing, many attributes needing parsimonious models, real-time quick-vs-accurate tradeoff.
High frequencies appear as false lower frequencies after undersampling, making perfect reconstruction impossible.
To remove frequencies above Nyquist and prevent aliasing.
Quantization is not reversible; rounding amplitudes into levels loses information.
More bits/rate gives lower distortion and better quality; fewer bits gives higher distortion and smaller files.
Luminance Y is brightness; chrominance Cb/Cr is color information.
The human eye is more sensitive to brightness than color, so chrominance can be reduced more with less perceived loss.
RGB → YCbCr → blocks → DCT → quantization → entropy coding.
Represents image blocks as weighted sums of cosine frequency components, concentrating energy in low-frequency coefficients.
Quantization.
Lossless coding that gives short codes to frequent symbols and longer codes to rare symbols.
JPEG compresses still images, mainly spatial redundancy. MPEG compresses video using spatial and temporal redundancy.
Encodes a frame by predicting it from other frames and storing only differences/motion information.
Motion estimation finds where blocks moved; motion compensation uses that motion to predict/reconstruct frames.
A set/sample of 3D points (x,y,z) representing the geometry of an object or scene.
A value/function defined on each node of a graph.
The Laplacian eigenvectors play the role of Fourier basis functions on a graph.
Low graph frequency: neighboring nodes similar. High graph frequency: neighboring nodes very different.
Dimensionality reduction by keeping directions/components with largest variance.
Eigenvectors are principal directions; eigenvalues measure variance explained along those directions.

multiple_choice_cards.question: A signal has maximum frequency W = 5 kHz. What is the minimum sampling frequency?
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: Nyquist says f_s ≥ 2W, so f_s ≥ 10 kHz.

multiple_choice_cards.question: If B = 8 bits, how many quantization levels are available?
What happens when LoRa spreading factor increases?
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: Higher SF gives longer symbol time and better sensitivity, but lower throughput.

multiple_choice_cards.question: Which MAC approach is used in wireless to avoid collisions?
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: Wireless cannot reliably detect collisions, so it tries to avoid them.

multiple_choice_cards.question: MQTT uses which communication model?
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: Clients publish to topics and subscribers receive messages via a broker.

multiple_choice_cards.question: In JPEG, which step is lossy?
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: DCT is a transform; entropy coding is lossless; quantization discards information.

multiple_choice_cards.question: In graph Fourier transform, what plays the role of Fourier basis functions?
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: The graph signal is expanded in the eigenvector basis of the graph Laplacian.

multiple_choice_cards.question: If SNR increases, what generally happens to BER?
choices: SNR has no relation to BER
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: A stronger signal relative to noise makes bit errors less likely.

multiple_choice_cards.question: What is a broadcast storm?
choices: Excessive/redundant rebroadcasts causing contention and collisions
multiple_choice_cards.answer: Excessive/redundant rebroadcasts causing contention and collisions
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: Broadcast storm is a major issue when many VANET nodes rebroadcast messages inefficiently.

multiple_choice_cards.question: Which list best matches the smart-environment data path?
choices: Acquisition → local processing → communication → stream → storage/cloud → analytics
multiple_choice_cards.answer: Acquisition → local processing → communication → stream → storage/cloud → analytics
multiple_choice_cards.explanation: This is the data path sequence from the intro slides.

cloze_cards.text: The graph Laplacian is L = {{c1::D - A}}.
LoRaWAN Class {{c1::A}} listens only after the end device transmits.
MQTT clients publish to {{c1::topics}} and a broker forwards messages to subscribers.
JPEG pipeline: RGB → YCbCr → blocks → {{c1::DCT}} → quantization → entropy coding.
In PCA, eigenvalues represent {{c1::variance explained}} by each principal component.

exam_sentences: A smart environment monitors physical conditions, processes data, makes decisions, and acts on the environment.
IoT is a network of physical objects embedded with sensors, electronics, software, and connectivity so they can collect/exchange data and react to commands.
The smart-environment data path is data acquisition, local processing, communication, stream, storage/cloud, and analytics.
LPWAN combines long range with low power and low data rate for IoT applications.
LoRa is the chirp-spread-spectrum physical modulation, while LoRaWAN is the network protocol and architecture built on top.
Increasing LoRa spreading factor improves sensitivity/range but decreases bitrate and increases time-on-air.
Sigfox is proprietary, ultra-narrowband, low-throughput, subscription-based LPWAN.
VANETs are vehicular ad hoc networks supporting V2V and V2I communication for safety and traffic efficiency.
CSMA/CA avoids collisions before transmission and is preferred in wireless where collision detection is difficult.
Broadcast storm occurs when redundant rebroadcasts cause contention and collisions.
MQTT is a lightweight publish/subscribe protocol where clients publish to topics and a broker forwards to subscribers.
WebSockets provide bidirectional full-duplex communication over a single TCP connection.
XMPP is an XML-based decentralized client-server protocol for near-real-time structured data exchange.
Cloud computing provides on-demand compute services over the Internet: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS, and security as a service.
Data analytics cleanses, transforms, and models data to discover useful information and support decisions.
Fourier transform represents a signal as a weighted sum of complex sinusoids.
Aliasing means high frequencies appear as false lower frequencies after undersampling.
DCT represents image blocks as weighted sums of cosine frequency components.
Quantization is the lossy step in JPEG.
Graph Fourier transform represents graph signals using Laplacian eigenvectors.

spaced_repetition_hint.priority_1: show twice as often as priority_2
spaced_repetition_hint.priority_3: show only after priority_1 and priority_2 are stable
